synthesis:
VERDICT: ARCHITECTURE. The bugs are symptoms; every confirmed latent defect is a re-instance of a class already root-fixed somewhere else in the fleet. Four structural generators:

R1. FORK-PER-VENUE WITH MANUAL PATCH PROPAGATION. 4 crypto bots = one codebase forked 4x (64.7% duplicated; xnn_core byte-identical 4x), fixes hand-ported with comment tags. Proven drift: _ensure_flat root-fix (2026-05-30) reached HL abort paths only — pac:504/ext:463/nado:600 still bare market_close; _sl_confirmed_live guard on 3/4 (nado missing); insert_pending on 1/4 (HL only); place-before-cancel trail on 3/4 (pacifica missing); per-asset leverage cap on 3/4 (nado missing); trail dead-code bug ran 20 extra days on 2 venues after extended fixed it; HL imports STALE private copies of sibling adapters; IB's perm-reject fix landed at 1 of 3 sibling sites in the same file. This generates the "fixes feel like half-measures" experience directly: every fix IS a half-measure by construction — it lands on 1 of 4-5 copies.
→ maps to: recurring-bugs class, knob-parity class, silent-failures class.

R2. NON-TRANSACTIONAL ENTRY LIFECYCLE + ONE-DIRECTIONAL RECONCILIATION. fill → SL → insert_trade ordering with DB-row-driven adoption means any death in the window (crash, OOM, watchdog os._exit — which R4 makes routine) creates a live position invisible to every safety net FOREVER on pac/ext/nado. HL solved it (insert_pending pre-send + startup pending-reconcile + untracked-protect sweep); never propagated (R1).
→ maps to: phantoms, naked-untracked positions, crash-recovery class.

R3. ADAPTER ERROR-CONTRACT INVERSION. Guards assume raise-on-failure; adapters mask failure into neutral values: open_positions → {}/stale-cache (all 4 crypto — makes the phantom-guard UNKNOWN branch unreachable: a 3-tick partial outage auto-closes ALL DB rows and CANCELS live SLs), market_close → ok-shaped error dict (discarded at 3 abort sites → naked), mark_price → 0.0 (fail-open breakout guard, phantom-unfilled on pacifica), IB cancel_order → ok unconfirmed, nado per-item parse drop → per-coin phantom-close. The contract is ambient convention, not enforced interface.
→ maps to: phantoms (the biggest single generator), silent-failures, fill-truth.

R4. SINGLE-THREADED LOOP + UNBOUNDED I/O + SUICIDE-WATCHDOG AS RECOVERY. Nado: zero timeouts on every SDK call incl. order send; IB: RequestTimeout never set, watchdog alerts-only (bot hangs until manual — 3 prior incidents); HL/pacifica WS feeds: ping_interval=0, no staleness reconnect → zombie feed silently reverts scans to serial throttled REST (the exact entry-latency class bought back 07-02, undetected). The watchdog kill lands mid-entry and FEEDS R2 (creates the untracked position).
→ maps to: falling-over, slow-execution, and (via kill-mid-entry) phantoms.

ONE-TIME STRUCTURAL FIX — target architecture (fleet_core package, venue adapters, IB kept separate):
A. Typed ExchangeClient interface, ONE implementation per venue, in one repo. Contract enforced, not documented: reads return verified data or raise ReadUnknown; close/cancel return only after position/order readback or raise. bare market_close REMOVED from the API — _ensure_flat becomes the only close primitive. Kills (impossible, not less likely): phantom mass-close-on-outage (UNKNOWN branch now reachable by construction), naked-after-abort (no unverified close exists to call), HL's stale adapter copies (single implementation), cancel-ok-unconfirmed.
B. Entry as persisted state machine: INTENT row pre-send → PENDING → FILLED → PROTECTED → OPEN; each transition idempotent; reconciler BIDIRECTIONAL (DB→exchange phantom-guard AND exchange→DB untracked-protect sweep with foreign fences, per-tick). Kills: crash-mid-entry invisible positions, fill→journal window, stale-oid naked (SL liveness is a state invariant re-verified per tick, not an oid null-check).
C. Transport layer with timeout-by-construction: HL's _install_session_timeout pattern generalized — every session/SDK client passes through one wrapper injecting (5s,20s); IB gets RequestTimeout + restart-capable in-process watchdog; WS feeds get nado's protocol-ping + a last_msg_age→force-reconnect watchdog with one WARNING on degrade. Kills: hung-fetch, silent WS-zombie latency regression, watchdog-suicide-mid-entry (calls bound in seconds, watchdog never needed for hangs).
D. Single conformance + chaos harness in CI: runs every adapter against injected timeout/429/partial-outage/empty-response/crash-at-each-state-transition, asserts the contract (raise not {}, flat-or-protected after abort, position recoverable from any kill point). Kills: R1 itself — a fix is a fix everywhere or CI is red; a new venue is an adapter + green harness, not a fork.
E. Independent invariant sentinel per venue (separate process): asserts every live position has a live reduce-only SL inside liq AND a DB row; every DB-open row has a position; orphan triggers fenced. Protect+alert on violation. Defense-in-depth for anything B misses.
F. One env schema, strict unknown-key rejection, one file per venue. Kills knob-parity/dual-env drift (nado .env vs .env.xnn, pacifica unit ambiguity, inert knobs).

MIGRATION (money never unprotected — exchange-resting SLs persist across every restart; each cutover is restart-shaped; shadow-compare before live):
Phase 0 — stop-the-bleed under current architecture, 2-3 agent-days, do FIRST: (1) _ensure_flat into abort paths pac/ext/nado (3 one-line-class edits); (2) nado SDK session timeouts (port exchange_hl.py:157); (3) IB RequestTimeout + watchdog restart action; (4) insert_pending + untracked-sweep port to pac/ext/nado; (5) open_positions raise-on-unknown on all 4; (6) _sl_confirmed_live on nado; (7) IB: patch remaining 2 confirm-fail sites + halt-defer for entries; (8) FIX-5 loud fence to pac/ext/nado. This closes every CRITICAL latent hole before any refactor risk is taken.
Phase 1 — extract byte-identical/99% modules into fleet_core (xnn_core, warmup_backfill, liquidity, strategy layer, risk, journal, orphan_sweep): 2 agent-days, provably zero behavior change (diff==empty), each venue flips imports one at a time.
Phase 2 — adapter interface + conformance/chaos harness against the 4 existing adapters; fix violations the harness finds: 4-5 agent-days. This is where R3 dies fleet-wide.
Phase 3 — extract exit/SL engine + entry state machine from trader.py (site of 4/7 drift bugs): 5-6 agent-days. Venue order: Extended first (strongest existing guards, 78% dup — lowest delta), Pacifica second (87% dup, vault account gains most safety), Nado third (most divergent, most latent bugs — biggest payoff, hardest port), HL LAST (canon donor, already safest, bespoke xyz leg stays venue-specific config). Each venue: 48h shadow run (parallel DRY process comparing every decision vs live bot) → cutover at flat-or-protected moment.
Phase 4 — main-loop scaffolding + WS feed core + invariant sentinel + env schema: 3 agent-days.
UNTOUCHED: IB architecture (not a fork sibling, 4% similarity, atomicity already server-side-correct) — IB gets only Phase 0 items + shared journal schema + harness membership. Strategies/risk numbers unchanged everywhere — this is execution plumbing only.
Total ≈ 16-19 agent-days; after Phase 0 (day 2-3) no known naked-money path remains open during the rest.

KEEP-PATCHING COST — confirmed latent bugs live TODAY (adversarially verified), each a booked future incident:
| bug | venue | money exposure |
|---|---|---|
| bare market_close on SL-fail-3x abort → naked UNTRACKED position | nado, pacifica (vault), extended | unbounded, invisible to all nets |
| crash/watchdog-kill between fill and insert_trade → untracked (naked if pre-SL) | pacifica, extended, nado | unbounded until manual audit |
| open_positions returns {} on outage → K=3 phantom-guard closes ALL rows + cancels live SLs | hl, pacifica, extended (+nado per-coin parse variant) | whole book naked in one 3-min partial outage |
| zero SDK timeouts, order-send included | nado | 5-8 min fleet-frozen per hang + kill-mid-entry feeds the above |
| no RequestTimeout, watchdog alert-only | ib | indefinite hang, no trailing/exits (3 prior incidents) |
| stale-oid naked: heal only on oid==None | nado | position naked forever after exchange-side trigger death |
| 2 of 3 confirm-fail sites still global-halt + skipped_kill consumes entries (both active sleeves) | ib | up to a month of lost tsmom38 exposure per glitch (lost 5 signals 07-01) |
| silent fence except-pass → sweep cancels manual/foreign SL | pacifica, extended (+nado dead FOREIGN fence) | manual position stripped of its stop |
| WS zombie, no liveness reconnect → silent serial-REST regression | hl, pacifica | entry-latency edge silently lost, undetected |
| fabricated fill readback (avgPx=mark, size=requested) → inert partial-fill guard, fake slip data | nado | sizing/parity corruption |
| tp1_full_exit / us29_max_hold record reference not fill | ib | parity/accounting corruption |

Run-rate under patching: incident history shows ~1 money-class event/week; every future fix costs 4-5 manual ports and empirically misses ≥1 sibling (7 documented drift cases). Patching spend exceeds the 16-19 day rebuild within ~2 months, and the naked-position tail risk (unbounded, on leveraged accounts) never closes.

censusVerdicts.cls: entry-serialization: blocking serial work (full-universe scan waits, per-coin throttles, serial WS-confirm waits, unbounded sleeps/fetches) between signal detection and order send, or blocking other entries while one is in flight (exits/SL-moves/heals on the same thread count)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Mostly fixed-once, one sibling latent. The streaming/enter-on-detect mechanism is a genuinely shared fix — the identical on_crypto_signal callback contract + shared _try_enter_signal path + immediate market-at-close entry is verified in code on all 4 scanning forks (hl/pacifica/extended/nado), default-ON, with the same 8-per-scan 429-safety cap; Extended's serial-WS-confirm variant got a mechanism rework (fail-fast + REST-confirm-by-external_id + trader-recreate heal), and IB is architecturally exempt with hard-bounded 3s confirms and persisted entry deferral. So the headline incident class is NOT whack-a-mole. BUT the second half of the entry-blocking mechanism — unbounded per-call HTTP waits on a single-threaded loop — was root-fixed only on HL (per-call SDK session timeouts); Pacifica/Extended happen to be safe natively; NADO still has zero per-call timeouts on every SDK call from scan through order send and leans on a 300s process-suicide watchdog, leaving a latent ~5-minute all-entries blackout window per hang. Action: propagate HL's _install_session_timeout pattern (or explicit per-call timeouts) into nado_protocol's engine/indexer/trigger clients to close the class 5/5.

censusVerdicts.cls: atomic-entry-sl: any path after an entry fill that leaves the position without a live SL on the exchange (SL-placement failure, exception, crash/restart, min-size rejection, silent close-miss), plus presence of emergency-close and post-placement readback
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole, with one genuinely shared mechanism. The per-tick SL-liveness readback + emergency-close guard ("never naked >1 tick") was propagated as a class fix to all four crypto forks and IB covers the class structurally (exchange-side bracket + 5-min reconciler auto-heal) — that sub-class is fixed once. But the 2026-05-30 root fix for silent close-miss (_ensure_flat: verified close + residual protective SL) reached HL's attempt_entry abort paths only; on pacifica (trader.py:486/504), extended (trader.py:445/463) and nado (trader.py:600) the SL-placement-failure emergency close is still a bare market_close whose error-dict return is discarded — each of these files even ships the fixed _ensure_flat and a docstring describing exactly this failure mode, applied to sibling paths but not here. Likewise the crash-mid-entry recovery mechanism (pre-order pending journal row + startup pending-reconcile + untracked-position protect sweep) exists ONLY on HL; on the other three crypto forks a crash between fill and insert_trade, or a missed emergency close, produces a naked position invisible to every safety net (adopt/reconcile read only DB rows). Net: 2/5 structurally prevented (hl, ib), 3/5 latent on the same two sibling paths HL already root-fixed — classic per-venue patch drift in a forked fleet.

censusVerdicts.cls: phantom-reconciliation: local trades.db/state vs live exchange divergence (missed fills, phantom DB rows, naked SL, symbol/dex mapping, reconciler false-positives)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Mixed - one sub-class fixed as a shared mechanism, two siblings still whack-a-mole. The false-phantom/heal-churn sub-class (xyz_NATGAS 2026-06-23) genuinely became a class fix: the identical phantom-guard block (module-level K=3 miss counter, cache-invalidated final re-read, real-fill exit lookup, close-DB-row-never-market-close) is ported verbatim to all 4 crypto forks, and IB's reconciler independently makes false-phantom non-destructive (halt, never auto-flat) with the 1OZ symbol-map fixed at the single canon table both readers share. BUT: (a) the crash-mid-entry pending-row fix ('write-db-row-PRE-order, panel must-fix 2026-06-21') exists ONLY on HL - pacifica/extended/nado all retain the fill->insert_trade crash window with NO detector for exchange-positions-without-DB-row (no untracked sweep, no phantom-at-exchange alert), so a missed-fill/crash there produces a permanently invisible, potentially naked position; (b) the per-tick SL-liveness readback (_sl_confirmed_live, 'never naked >1 tick', 2026-06-07) reached hl/pacifica/extended but never nado, which still heals only on oid-is-None. Fleet-wide design residual: all 4 crypto exchange clients swallow open_positions errors and return {}/stale-cache instead of raising, making the phantom-guard's 'read UNKNOWN -> defer' branch unreachable - a fetch failure immediately after the final-re-read cache invalidation reads as confirmed absence. Reconciler cadence: crypto = every main-loop tick (in-process adopt + phantom-guard + debounced 180s orphan sweep); IB = startup-synchronous + 5-min systemd timer.

censusVerdicts.cls: timeouts: outbound calls (HTTP/WS/SDK/IB-API) without explicit bounds, retry loops that can spin forever, threads that hang with no watchdog (the HL SDK hung-fetch class)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole with one honest exception. The DETECTION layer (loop-progress watchdog, os._exit -> systemd restart) did propagate as a shared mechanism to all 4 crypto bots — but it converts a permanent hang into a 8-30-min blind stall + hard restart, it does not prevent the class. The actual ROOT fix (inject a default timeout on the SDK's HTTP session) exists on exactly one venue (hl exchange_hl.py:157). Its siblings: nado carries the byte-identical latent defect (nado_protocol SDK sessions post/get with no timeout, including the entry-order and trigger/SL execute paths — a stall mid-entry can leave a filled position naked until watchdog-restart + reconcile); ib has unbounded ib_insync blocking calls (RequestTimeout never set) with NO restart-capable watchdog at all — alert-only, hang persists until manual intervention, despite two prior hang incidents on this venue; pacifica survives on per-call-site discipline (all current calls bounded) with the natural injection choke point (its session.request wrapper) left unused. Extended independently reached a structural fix (mandatory bridge timeout on every call). Fix-once verdict: NO — to close the class, port session-timeout injection to nado (SDK sessions), set IB.RequestTimeout + add an in-process stall watchdog on ib, and add default-timeout injection to pacifica's existing wrapper.

censusVerdicts.cls: ws-lifecycle (reconnect, staleness detection, re-subscribe after reconnect, rate-limit handling, blocking confirm-waits)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Split verdict. The MONEY-safety half of the class is fixed once via a genuinely shared mechanism: all four crypto forks treat WS strictly as a cache with a read-time finality/staleness gate and automatic REST fallback ('any WS failure -> store stale -> get_df None -> REST'), so no ws-lifecycle failure can serve wrong bars or lose an order silently (Extended additionally makes REST the truth for fills). The LIVENESS half is whack-a-mole: connection keepalive + dead-socket reconnect exists only in Nado's feed (protocol ping/ping_timeout); HL and Pacifica run run_forever(ping_interval=0) with ping errors swallowed and no watchdog on _last_msg_ts, so a zombie WS silently and permanently re-opens the just-fixed entry-latency defect (serial 1.5s/coin REST scans) with zero alerting; Extended's feed delegates liveness to unverified SDK internals with DEBUG-level error logging. Likewise the WS-confirm fail-fast+REST-confirm rework lives only in the extended fork's monkey-patch, while hl and pacifica still carry dormant UNPATCHED copies of the same exchange_extended.py adapter (stock 5s WS wait + waiter leak) selectable via EXCHANGE=extended — classic fork-drift knob/patch-parity gap. Recommended class closure: port Nado-style protocol keepalive + a last_msg_age->force-reconnect watchdog (with one WARNING on degrade) to hl/pacifica/extended feeds, and either delete or patch-sync the legacy extended adapters in hl/pacifica.

censusVerdicts.cls: silent-failures (bare except / except-log-continue / return-None-on-error on money paths; failsafe-on-every-item = silent feature disable; errors at INFO/DEBUG)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole with disciplined hand-porting, not a fixed class. Several sub-classes WERE closed fleet-wide after single-venue incidents (false-empty trigger list → RAISE on all 4 crypto venues; phantom-fill 'unparseable ≠ filled' confirm; open_positions failure → stale-cache/presence-UNKNOWN; margin_used_usd raises instead of returning 0; scanner all-items-skipped FAIL-LOUD on all 5) — but each is a comment-tagged manual port ('canon §0#10', 'XNN port 2026-06-11') across 5 separate forks with no shared library, no lint/CI gate banning except-pass on money paths, and no fleet parity check. Proven drift confirms the mole pattern: fence fail-loud (FIX-5) exists ONLY on HL while pacifica/extended/nado retain the exact silently-dead-fence except-pass it fixed; orphan-sweep fail-streak escalation exists ONLY on nado while the other three can skip the safety sweep every cycle forever at WARNING; per-item position-parse swallow survives ONLY on nado; IB's kill-halt entry consumption is still terminal in this snapshot. Nothing is structurally_prevented anywhere — the mechanism (forked code + manual propagation) guarantees siblings stay latent.

censusVerdicts.cls: halt-semantics: when a guard fires (kill switch, perm-reject, margin/risk gate), is the skip scoped to the one bad item or global, and does the blocked signal recover after the condition clears or get terminally consumed
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole on IB, structurally clean on the four crypto forks — but for opposite reasons. The crypto forks never grew halt machinery at all: every guard is a per-signal reject into a journal with full re-evaluation each scan, so the class (global halt + terminal signal consumption + no recovery) has no substrate to fire on; their only residual is the edge-triggered bar latch (transient guard failure eats ONE bar's signals, bounded + logged), with a fork divergence in latch ordering (hl peek-then-commit vs pacifica/extended/nado consume-on-check). IB — the one bot WITH kill/halt machinery — is mid-whack-a-mole: the perm-reject→global-halt root-fix was applied at exactly 1 of 3 sibling bracket-confirm sites in order_router.py (the two UNpatched sites serve both currently-active sleeves tsmom38/us29), and the halt-eats-entries fix is still only a drafted relay patch — the three skipped_kill terminal-consumption sites in trader.py are live as of this snapshot. Class NOT fixed once: no shared confirm-fail handler exists (three copy-pasted blocks), so the next perm-reject on a tsmom38/us29 entry re-triggers the global halt AND terminally eats every remaining entry signal of that cycle.

censusVerdicts.cls: fill-truth: entries/exits recorded from ACTUAL exchange fill (price+size readback) vs intended/reference price; exit reasons oid-first vs guessed (wick_sl SL-ref x1330, 'manual' mislabel incidents)
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole with partial convergence, not a shared mechanism. The exit-PRICE half of the class was propagated fleet-wide as a copy-pasted readback block (all 4 crypto forks' _emergency_close + phantom-guard read real fill VWAP; IB added a conditional guard) — that wing is closed. But because the fleet is 5 separate forks there is no single choke-point library, and each sub-wing landed unevenly: OID-FIRST exit attribution exists ONLY on HL (siblings still price-window-±1% and dump off-ref bot-SL fills to 'unknown_investigate' — honest, no longer a 'manual' lie, but the attribution root-fix was not propagated; extended's adapter doesn't even export order ids); Nado never had entry fill-truth at all (avgPx synthesized from mark, totalSz echoed as requested size → inert partial-fill guard + fabricated slip metrics, despite real entryPx being available in its own open_positions); IB's guard keys on exit_px<=0 instead of routing EVERY close through fill readback, so tp1_full_exit and us29_max_hold still record reference prices today. Net: 1/5 structurally prevented (HL), 2/5 adhoc-patched with the label wing open, 2/5 with concrete latent code paths where the class still fires.

censusVerdicts.cls: crash-recovery: state lost on kill -9 (trail levels, pending/in-flight entries, cooldowns, time-stop clocks) + whether startup re-arm is verified against the live exchange
censusVerdicts.verdict: Whack-a-mole. The class has been genuinely solved TWICE, independently — HL (pending-row-pre-submit + startup pending-reconcile + untracked-protect sweep + restore-grace SL verify vs live exchange + ATR_e/entry_bar_ts persistence) and IB (state_manager SQLite for all runtime state incl. trail_sl + synchronous startup reconciler that verifies every position/order against live IB and halts on drift + atomic server-side brackets) — but neither shared mechanism was propagated to the three crypto forks. Pacifica/Extended/Nado each received only spot patches (trail-SL DB persistence 07-01 on all three; per-tick SL-liveness guard on pacifica+extended only). Concrete siblings still latent: (1) crash-mid-entry fill->insert_trade window on ALL THREE leaves a live, possibly naked, position with no DB row and no startup mechanism that can ever find it; (2) Nado uniquely lacks the per-tick SL-liveness guard, so a dead sl_order_id restored from DB (e.g. kill -9 inside _handle_tp1_partial's cancel-before-place) is a permanently naked position; (3) restart resets the 120-bar time-stop clock on all three (only HL persists entry_bar_ts). Startup re-arm verified against live exchange: HL yes (restore-grace reconcile), IB yes (reconciler), pacifica/extended partially (per-tick liveness guard converges within one tick), nado NO (DB oid trusted blindly).

maps.bot: HL combo/valantis bot (hl_bot_v2 framework, dual-leg: donchian-8h crypto + us29 F4_vb xyz_ HIP-3 stocks; .env.combo DRY_RUN=0, LOOP_INTERVAL_SEC=60, RESTING_ORDERS_ENABLE=0, HL_WS_CANDLE=1, LEG_SPLIT_SCAN=1)
maps.entry_flow: Single-threaded 60s loop (bot/main.py:1112 while True) with boundary-aligned wake: sleep shrinks to ~0.5s polls in the first 15s after any TF bar boundary (main.py:1461-1478), so bar-close detect lag ~2s not 60s. Scan is bar-close-latched per TF (scanner.py:115-134, peek/commit so SCAN_BAR_DELAY never drops a bar). Legs split and crypto-first (main.py:1100-1111, LEG_SPLIT_SCAN=1 + CRYPTO_LEG_FIRST default 1): crypto 8h walk runs BEFORE the ~200s xyz walk. Crypto signals STREAM into entry on-detect mid-scan via on_crypto_signal callback (scanner.py:432-436 -> main.py:1389-1396 _try_enter_signal), capped CRYPTO_STREAM_MAX_PER_SCAN=8 (main.py:1339) — this is the 2026-07-02 entry-latency fix (no longer waits for full universe walk). xyz/us29 stays batch (topM needs the full list, main.py:1421-1449). Candle throttles only on REST cache-miss: 0.5s main / 1.5s HIP-3 + jitter warm, 1.8/3.0s cold (exchange_hl.py:579-586); WS push store serves scans with <=3s ready-wait (exchange_hl.py:553-563) + background pre-warm thread (main.py:1049-1085) so warm-path scans are ~0 REST. Blocking waits inside an entry: xyz liq-px poll up to 6x1s (trader.py:733-752), SL-live confirm poll <=~7.5s (trader.py:962-974); crypto poll=1 keeps its latency flat. Loop-progress watchdog force-exits the process after max(1800s, 30x interval) of no progress (main.py:561-576, armed :783).
maps.order_path: Sequence per entry (attempt_entry, trader.py:121): gates (bar-age :139, tier :154, equity :162, concurrent cap :179, size/liq cap :206-282, MM-cap vs LIVE exchange margin fail-closed :284-313) -> update_leverage set on exchange pre-order, abort if refused (:319-334; cross for crypto, isolated for xyz_ :327) -> journal 'pending' row BEFORE submit (:405-412, crash-recoverable) -> breakout-validity mark-band guard (:434-444) -> MARKET order with 0.5% SDK slippage cap (:451, exchange_hl.py:1281-1299); cap-breach/partial-fill (<10%) => _ensure_flat + 900s re-entry cooldown (:1117-1139, :66); market_open exception/unparseable response => positions-poll confirm before declaring unfilled (:1096-1102, :1151-1179 — 2026-07-02 hung-fetch propagation) -> ensure_sl_inside_liq (REMEDY-A add isolated margin / REMEDY-B clamp, :505-511, :680-836) -> SL trigger reduce-only, 3 retries (:515-521, _place_sl_with_retry :881-928, every oid registered to the bot-own registry :921) -> post-invariant SL-inside-liq else cancel SL + emergency close (:525-545) -> SL fail 3x => emergency close, never naked (:546-569) -> promote pending->open with actual fill (:577-591). TP: partial-TP maker limit code exists (:649-675) but TP1_PARTIAL_FRAC=0 in .env.combo => no TP order; exits are chandelier trail + 120-bar time-stop (donchian) / chandelier-7 (us29). NOT atomic, but every gap is fail-closed: crash mid-entry recovered by _reconcile_pending at startup (main.py:180-224); readback: SL liveness confirmed against the exchange EVERY tick (_sl_confirmed_live trader.py:931-959) with heal-or-emergency-close (:1527-1579) bounding any naked window to <=1 tick; trail re-place is place-before-cancel (:1797-1832) with post-invariant (:1808-1819).
maps.state_model: Hybrid: sqlite trades.db is bookkeeping (journal.py:52-136; pending->open->closed rows, persisted sl/tp oids, frozen atr14 + entry_bar_ts for restart-exact trails), the EXCHANGE is truth for presence/margin/SL-liveness. Reconciliation layers: (1) startup pending-row reconcile promotes crash-mid-entry fills / deletes stale (main.py:180-224); (2) startup restore + PER-TICK adopt of DB-open rows live on exchange (main.py:227-392, called each tick :1157-1166), winning-row selection by live-SL oid when a coin has duplicate rows (:148-177), startup-blip retry when exchange reads empty (:266-275); (3) restore-grace one-time SL reconcile that re-protects, never closes, on a stale 'through' DB SL (trader.py:1331-1398); (4) phantom-guard: module-level K=3 consecutive-miss counter + final fresh re-read before auto-closing a DB row absent from live positions, real close px looked up from user_fills with oid-first attribution (trader.py:76-77, 1449-1518, 1969-2035); (5) startup untracked-protect sweep places liq-guarded ±6% SL on any DB-less SL-less position, with MANUAL_POSITION_PREFIXES=xyz_ fence + UNRECONCILED critical when own-vs-manual unprovable (main.py:836-928); (6) per-tick orphan reduce-only trigger sweep, oid-registry fenced, 180s debounce (main.py:1198-1206, orphan_sweep.py); (7) positions/user_state/mids/fills caches 20s/10s/5s/60s with explicit invalidation after fills (exchange_hl.py:312-327, 889-902).
maps.error_handling: 236 `except Exception` across bot/*.py (trader.py 48, main.py 38, exchange_hl.py 30), zero bare `except:`; ~10 `except Exception: pass` confined to best-effort cancels/registry appends (e.g. trader.py:1386, :923). Dominant pattern: catch-log-continue with a DOCUMENTED fail direction per site. Money gates fail-CLOSED: MM-cap margin read failure rejects the entry when positions exist (trader.py:291-303); list_open_sl_orders/list_reduce_only_triggers RAISE on any dex query failure so the caller's assume-live/skip-cycle fail-safe engages (exchange_hl.py:1503-1530, 1471-1475); account_value raises after retries (exchange_hl.py:774) -> entry skipped. Reads fail-OPEN only where safe-by-design and loud: regime gate ON on fetch error with REGIME-INERT ERROR alarm (selector_us29.py:180-186, :204-222); wick-phantom check closes on any read failure (trader.py:1846-1849). One systemic exception to the discipline: exchange_hl.open_positions() swallows terminal failures into stale-cache-or-{} instead of raising (exchange_hl.py:841-851) — see structural risk #1.
maps.timeout_coverage: All REST/HTTP paths have explicit timeouts: SDK Info/Exchange requests.Session monkey-patched with hard (connect=5s, read=20s) on EVERY request (exchange_hl.py:157-184, installed :243 and :277 — the 2026-07-02 hung-fetch root-fix, covers candles/all_mids/user_state/order/cancel/info.post); raw requests.post timeout=10 (exchange_hl.py:114, :338, :394-397); universe.py:119 timeout=_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SEC; warmup_backfill.py:115 urlopen timeout=12 (self-documented caveat :108-110: per-read timeout, a slow-trickle response can extend it — warmup path only, watchdog-bounded). NOT covered: the WS candle feed reader — ws_candle_feed.py:82 run_forever(ping_interval=0) has no read/pong timeout and the app-level ping loop (:92-99) never checks liveness, so a half-open TCP can block the reader thread forever with no reconnect; hot path is protected by the _last_msg_ts<5s liveness gate + REST fallback in candles() (exchange_hl.py:553-563), so this degrades speed, not safety. The loop-progress watchdog (main.py:561-576) backstops any residual main-thread hang.

structural_risks.issue: open_positions() NEVER raises — on sustained fetch failure it returns stale cache or, if the cache was just invalidated, an EMPTY {} (return pc[1] if pc else {}). The phantom-guard's contract explicitly distinguishes 'read UNKNOWN (open_positions threw)' from confirmed-absent (trader.py:1459-1469), but the adapter never throws: a {} from a swallowed user_state outage counts as CONFIRMED-ABSENT. After K=3 manage ticks (~3 min) the guard auto-closes the DB row AND cancels the position's live reduce-only SL via _cancel_orphan_triggers (trader.py:1497-1515) — while the position is still live on the exchange.
structural_risks.money_impact: Scenario: any fill calls invalidate_positions_cache() (trader.py:493, main.py:1327), then HL user_state fails non-429 for >=3 ticks while frontendOpenOrders still answers (partial-outage shape seen Jun 25 stall burst) -> EVERY open position is phantom-closed in DB, its SL cancelled, and it rides NAKED and UNTRACKED (closed DB row -> per-tick adopt can't recover; untracked-protect sweep is startup-only, main.py:836). Unbounded downside until restart/sentinel. Same phantom/naked class as xyz_NATGAS and IB 1OZ incidents — the K=3 guard is defeated because the UNKNOWN branch is unreachable.

structural_risks.issue: Phantom-guard 'final fresh re-read' blip protection is defeated by the same swallow contract: it wraps client.open_positions() in try/except expecting a raise on failure (trader.py:1486-1490), but a failed fetch after invalidate_positions_cache() (trader.py:1483) returns {} normally, which confirms absence and proceeds to auto-close + SL cancel.
structural_risks.money_impact: The single safety valve designed to prevent a false auto-close at tick K relies on an exception path the HL adapter never takes; one transient read failure at the confirm instant converts a live protected position into a naked untracked one. Fix belongs in the adapter (raise on no-data instead of returning {}), else both sites stay latent.

structural_risks.issue: F4_vb 'top-3 of the DAY' is enforced per SCAN-CYCLE, and the daily backstop is off: select_topm slices per cycle (main.py:1437, selector_us29.py:241-252), the us29 leg scans BOTH 4h and 1d (strategy_us29.py:127-130 US29_TF_CONFIG), and MAX_OPENS_PER_DAY=0 in .env.combo:32 disables the counter gate (main.py:1346) that the env comment itself describes as the F4_vb defense-in-depth daily cap.
structural_risks.money_impact: Up to 7 selection cycles/day x top-3 = up to 21 xyz opens/day vs the validated 3/day cell — xyz concentration can exceed the backtested config multi-fold in a hot tape; only MM-cap 50% bounds it. Validated-config drift = un-modeled DD risk (us29 bt maxDD math assumed 3/day).

structural_risks.issue: WS candle feed has no dead-socket detection: run_forever(ping_interval=0) disables protocol ping/pong (ws_candle_feed.py:82), the app-level ping loop is fire-and-forget (:92-99), and reconnect happens only when run_forever RETURNS (:73-90) — a half-open TCP blocks the reader forever with no staleness watchdog to force-close/reconnect.
structural_risks.money_impact: Feed goes silently dark; candles() liveness gate falls back to throttled REST (1.5s/coin HIP-3, exchange_hl.py:584) -> scans stretch back to minutes + 429 storms — the exact entry-latency class fixed 2026-07-02 re-emerges silently; loop watchdog will NOT fire (loop still progresses). Late entries = slippage/missed fills, not naked risk.

structural_risks.issue: _confirm_fill_via_positions records the entry at MARK price, not the actual fill VWAP from user_fills (trader.py:1064-1072), after a market_open read-timeout/unparseable response.
structural_risks.money_impact: Journaled entry/slip/realized-R are approximations for such fills (R denominator uses entry-sl_initial, trader.py:2042-2048); SL itself is placed from signal.sl_price so money-safety holds — parity/reporting distortion only.

maps.state_model: Hybrid: exchange is truth for positions, SQLite trades.db (journal.py:37-110, WAL-less, commit-per-op) is truth for trade identity/SL intent. structural_risks.issue: Confirmed fill + mark_price()==0 => naked UNTRACKED position: after an unparseable market_open response, position is confirmed live via open_positions (szi>0) but the return is gated on mark>0; a failed /info/prices read (returns 0.0, exchange_pacifica.py:641-651 - correlated with the API stress that made the order response unparseable) falls through to 'treating as UNFILLED' -> attempt_entry inserts rejected, no DB row, NO SL ever placed. DB-row-driven adopt (main.py:126) never picks it up; foreign-collision gate (trader.py:178) blocks re-entry but nothing heals it.
structural_risks.money_impact: Unprotected leveraged position invisible to bot and DB; unbounded loss until manual audit. Same code path duplicated at trader.py:1189 (_place_and_wait_fill).

structural_risks.issue: open_positions() violates the raise-on-failure contract the phantom-guard depends on: on API failure it returns stale cache or {} (exchange_pacifica.py:392-405), so trader.py:1246's except-branch (presence UNKNOWN) never fires for Pacifica; a >=3-tick (~3 min) API outage starting when the positions cache is empty (post-invalidate or post-restart) yields {} reads counted as CONFIRMED-ABSENT, and the K=3 final re-read (which itself invalidates the cache, trader.py:1264) also returns {} -> auto-closes ALL tracked DB rows and calls _cancel_orphan_triggers on live positions' real SLs.
structural_risks.money_impact: During a partial outage (GET /orders up, /positions down) the live protective SL gets cancelled -> naked position; full outage -> DB rows falsely closed at fabricated exit px, positions untracked with frozen trail. IB-symbol-map/HIP-3 false-phantom sibling class.

structural_risks.issue: Crash window between market fill and insert_trade: journal row is written only after ensure_sl_inside_liq + SL placement + invariant checks (~1-3s of REST calls, trader.py:445-521); a crash/OOM/watchdog _os._exit in that window leaves a live exchange position with (maybe) a resting SL but NO DB row - restart adopt is DB-driven so it is never re-tracked, never trailed.
structural_risks.money_impact: Position rides with static (or zero) SL indefinitely; no doubles (foreign-collision gate blocks re-entry) but trail/TP management lost - PnL leak on every crash-at-entry.

structural_risks.issue: Trail SL replace is cancel-then-place with no immediate readback: old stop cancelled (trader.py:1468) before new trigger_sl; if placement returns error, new_sl_oid=None is stored and the position is naked until next tick's class-guard heal; inverse failure (cancel fails, place succeeds) leaves TWO resting stops until the 180s-debounced orphan sweep.
structural_risks.money_impact: ~60s naked window per trail failure (crash inside window extends to restart+1 tick); duplicate reduce-only stop is clamped so over-close is impossible - bounded but recurring exposure on every trailing bar.

structural_risks.issue: _lookup_real_close_px attributes exits from user_fills() served from a 60s-TTL cache (exchange_pacifica.py:1273-1291) with no filter against the trade's open time: a just-fired SL may be missing from the cached window, or older same-coin Close fills (last 200) get summed as this position's exit.
structural_risks.money_impact: Wrong exit_price/pnl/realized_r/exit_reason in trades.db (phantom-guard and emergency-close paths) - corrupts parity/exec-slip accounting, no live-order impact. wick_sl-records-ref-price sibling class, partially fixed but cache-staleness residue remains.

structural_risks.issue: WS candle feed has no pong-deadline/read-timeout: run_forever(ping_interval=0) with a separate fire-and-forget ping loop (ws_candle_feed.py:90-97); a half-open TCP connection blocks recv forever, no reconnect ever triggers, feed goes permanently stale with only a debug-level stats() surface.

structural_risks.issue: 429 retry in _signed_request sleeps in-line up to 30s x2 attempts, and kline 429 sleeps ~5s, all on the single main thread - position management for every other coin stalls behind one rate-limited call (watchdog threshold 480s never trips).
structural_risks.money_impact: SL heal/trail/exit decisions delayed up to ~90s during 429 storms - exactly when the exchange is stressed; bounded by resting exchange-side stops.

Per tick: manage positions → reconcile DB rows → orphan sweep → scan. Scan gated on new_bar_closed per TF (scanner.py:94), so entries only at bar boundaries by design. Entry-latency streaming fix (2026-07-02 fleet port) IS present: on_crypto_signal callback enters each signal the instant the scanner detects it, mid-scan, capped 8/scan (main.py:485-581, CRYPTO_STREAM_ENTRY default on), fallback batch loop after (main.py:585-587). Candle throttle: REST indexer paced NADO_FETCH_SLEEP_SEC (default 0.25s/coin, exchange_nado.py:497) with 3x exp backoff on 429/timeout (exchange_nado.py:500-516), but WS push feed (NADO_WS_CANDLE=1 in .env.xnn) serves closed bars from memory with time-based finality — 0 indexer calls on hot path (ws_candle_feed.py:224-269, main.py:375-396). Blocking waits are all bounded and short: SL readback ≤~3s (trader.py:749), phantom-fill confirm ≤4s (trader.py:1011-1022), _ensure_flat ≤~9s (trader.py:889-906). No 30s-serial-WS-confirm class. Loop-progress watchdog force-exits the process after ≥480s no-progress (main.py:301-324,401) — heartbeat beats only once per iteration (main.py:428).
maps.order_path: Gates: bar-age (trader.py:322) → tier (337) → equity (344) → concurrent cap vs LIVE exchange positions (356-364, cap=999 non-binding) → size = min(risk, leverage, 5% of 1h-vol snapshot) with $20 floor (374-449) → MM-cap vs LIVE SDK MarginManager margin, fail-CLOSED on read error (457-491) → breakout-validity live-mark guard (514-523) → 15-min re-entry cooldown (531). Send: ONE market FOK, oracle-anchored in-band limit built manually (2028 fix) (trader.py:543 → exchange_nado.py:1049-1145; isolated-mode fallback :1150-1235). Post-fill sequence: invalidate positions cache (568) → SL clamped inside liquidation +2% buffer (ensure_sl_inside_liq, 577; liq px computed from maint health, exchange_nado.py:709-758) → SL trigger placed with 3 retries + DOUBLE readback (exchange-level _confirm_trigger_live matches live trigger service, exchange_nado.py:1277-1337, fabricated-oid path deleted :1740-1771; trader-level _sl_live_on_exchange re-verify, trader.py:721-757, raise=INDETERMINATE=assume-live) → SL fail 3x ⇒ emergency market_close + reject (594-614) → journal insert_trade ONLY AFTER SL rests (617) → post-place invariant: SL must be inside liq or emergency-close (654) → best-effort TP1 (off live, TP1_PARTIAL_FRAC=0). NOT atomic: fill→SL→journal is a 3-step sequence; crash between fill and journal = untracked position (see risks). Trail updates are place-NEW-before-cancel-OLD (trader.py:1427-1450, never-naked); TP1-partial path is still cancel-then-place (849-859) with emergency-close remediation. Fill readback gap: on a parseable response the entry price/size are FABRICATED by the wrapper (avgPx=mark, totalSz=requested, exchange_nado.py:1720-1736); only unparseable responses trigger position-readback confirm (trader.py:1008-1037).
maps.state_model: Three layers: exchange = authority for position presence + margin (open_positions from subaccount summary, 20s TTL cache, exchange_nado.py:663-703; margin/equity via SDK MarginManager, RAISE on failure :618-651); sqlite trades.db = authority for trade params across restarts (journal.py:37-110, sl_current persisted on every trail, trader.py:1453); in-memory dict = working set. Heal/reconcile mechanics: startup restore DB→memory gated on exchange presence with 3x anti-blip retry (main.py:76-99); per-tick reconciler adopts DB-open rows into memory incl. pending-entry placeholders (main.py:146-187); phantom-guard = module-level K=3 consecutive-miss counter + cache-invalidated final re-read before auto-close, real exit px looked up from per-product matches (trader.py:1219-1287, 1559-1611); naked-SL heal with emergency-close fallback (trader.py:1289-1313); orphan reduce-only trigger sweep, 180s debounce, fenced coins exempt, indeterminate-view skip (orphan_sweep.py:118-250); stale-positions-cache fallback on summary failure to prevent phantom-close, unbounded age (exchange_nado.py:670-682). STRUCTURAL GAP: reconciliation is one-directional — exchange positions with NO DB row are never adopted (no exchange→DB heal), see risk #1.
maps.error_handling: 135 `except Exception` clauses across bot/ (trader.py 41, exchange_nado.py 40, main.py 14, orphan_sweep 9, others 31); 0 bare `except:`. Dominant pattern = catch-log-continue on read/enumeration paths, with DELIBERATE fail-loud/fail-closed on money paths: margin_used_usd/equity_with_upnl RAISE (exchange_nado.py:634,650 → entry refused, trader.py:457-481); SL enumeration RAISES instead of returning [] (exchange_nado.py:916,1543 — false-naked class fix); trigger-client-None escalates to TriggerClientUnavailable → ERROR/CRITICAL (exchange_nado.py:824-857, orphan_sweep.py:134-143); orphan sweep skip-cycle + WARNING→ERROR escalation after 10 fails (orphan_sweep.py:144-154); universe fetch RAISES (universe.py:110-117); scanner all-coins-empty FAIL-LOUD (scanner.py:240-246); insane-price guard refuses order sends (exchange_nado.py:214-228). Residual soft spots: attempt_entry SL-fail path ignores market_close return (risk #3); trail-stage compute swallows exceptions silently (strategy_xnn.py:644-645, raise-only so stall-safe).
maps.timeout_coverage: NO — SDK calls have no explicit timeouts. Covered: warmup_backfill HTTP (urlopen timeout=12 + 30s total slow-trickle deadline, bot/warmup_backfill.py:87-102); WS candle feed (open_timeout=15, ping_timeout=10, recv wait_for 60s, bot/ws_candle_feed.py:131-141). NOT covered — every nado_protocol SDK HTTP call runs on the SDK's default session with no timeout: get_candlesticks (exchange_nado.py:502), get_engine_subaccount_summary (:578), list_trigger_orders (:885), get_subaccount_multi_products_open_orders (:927), get_market_price (:967), get_perp_prices (:997), place_order entry FOK (:1133, :1230), close_position (:1252), place_price_trigger_order SL/TP (:1372, :1430), cancel_orders/cancel_trigger_orders (:1472, :1498), get_matches (:1577), get_market_liquidity (:1096), plus liquidity_snapshot/universe fetches through the same client. The HL hung-fetch root-fix (hard 5s/20s session timeout, project_hl_sdk_hung_fetch_timeout_rootfix 2026-07-02) is NOT ported here; the only mitigation is the process-level loop watchdog that force-exits after ≥480s of no loop progress (main.py:301-324,401) — a band-aid that itself widens the non-atomic-entry crash window.

structural_risks.issue: Untracked-position adoption gap: fill→journal is non-atomic (market fill at trader.py:543, insert_trade only at :617 AFTER SL placement) and there is NO exchange→DB reconciler — startup restore adopts only DB rows (main.py:95-99), per-tick reconciler is DB→memory only (main.py:146-187). A crash or watchdog _os._exit(1) (main.py:321) between fill and journal leaves a live position invisible to the bot FOREVER: no SL heal, no management. Worse, same-coin dedup is in-memory only (main.py:494), so a later signal on that coin re-enters and Nado MERGES same-product positions per subaccount → the new SL covers only the new size.
structural_risks.money_impact: Naked or partially-protected position with unbounded downside until manual discovery; hang-during-SL-placement + watchdog kill is the realistic trigger (SDK calls have no timeouts).

structural_risks.issue: No HTTP timeout on ANY nado_protocol SDK call (entry FOK :1133, SL trigger :1372, cancels :1472/1498, summary :578, candles :502, trigger list :885). The hung-fetch class root-fixed on HL (hard 5s/20s session timeout) is NOT ported to this venue; sole mitigation is the loop watchdog force-exit after >=480s stall (main.py:308-324), during which SL trailing/healing for ALL positions is frozen, and whose kill mid-entry feeds the critical risk above.
structural_risks.money_impact: Up to 8 minutes of frozen SL management per hang; process kill mid-entry creates untracked naked positions.

structural_risks.issue: attempt_entry SL-fail emergency close discards the market_close() result (trader.py:600): market_close reports a still-open position via an error status INSIDE an ok-shaped dict (exchange_nado.py:1259-1265), not an exception — a silently-failed close leaves a naked UNTRACKED position (insert_rejected, no DB row). The managed-position path uses _ensure_flat (retry 3x + protective SL on residual, trader.py:873-923); this path doesn't — intra-file mechanism parity gap.
structural_risks.money_impact: SL placement failed 3x + close silently missed = naked position with no journal row and no restart recovery.

structural_risks.issue: Fabricated fill readback: _wrap_open_resp synthesizes avgPx = mark_price and totalSz = requested size for every non-raising order response (exchange_nado.py:1720-1736). Trader's cap-breach guard (trader.py:976) and min_fill_ratio guard (:988) therefore evaluate fabricated numbers and are structurally INERT on Nado (silent-disable class — the guards can never fire on a real bad fill); journaled entry price is mark, not fill VWAP, so walk_slip_pct/realized_r are approximations. Position-readback confirm runs only on the UNPARSEABLE-response branch (trader.py:1008-1037).
structural_risks.money_impact: Bad/partial fills undetected at entry (bounded by the oracle-anchored FOK ±0.5% band); PnL/R accounting drift vs true fills.

structural_risks.issue: SL trigger executes as a LIMIT at trigger±0.5% (hardcoded slip=0.005, exchange_nado.py:1352-1353). A gap through the trigger by >0.5% can leave the triggered limit unfilled (thin Ink-L2 books; 10.10-crash class). Backstop is the 60s bot-loop check_sl_hit → emergency market close (trader.py:1379-1399) — absent entirely if the process is down.
structural_risks.money_impact: Stop miss on violent gaps; loss beyond -1R up to next loop tick, unbounded if bot dead.

structural_risks.issue: Foreign-merge fence OFF: bot trades on shared subaccount 'default' (NADO_SUBACCOUNT=default) where Nado merges same-product positions; UNIVERSE_SYMBOL_EXCLUDE is EMPTY in both .env and .env.xnn, and attempt_entry has no same-coin-on-exchange guard (trader.py:354-364 only counts positions for the cap). A signal on a coin holding a manual/legacy position silently averages into it; SL sized only for the bot's own fill.
structural_risks.money_impact: Bot entry merges into a foreign position → wrong-size SL, partial protection, entry-price accounting corrupted.

structural_risks.issue: Dual env-file knob source: config.py loads ./.env unconditionally (config.py:20-21, mtime Jun 21) while active tuning lives in .env.xnn (mtime Jun 27, presumably via live systemd EnvironmentFile — snapshot's systemd/nado-bot-v2.service is stale May 24, no EnvironmentFile, so unverifiable here). Keys DIVERGE now: .env has SHORT_TFS=4h / SCAN_CANDLES_LIMIT=3000 / UNIVERSE_MIN_VOL_USD_24H=500000 / DONCHIAN_TFS=8h vs .env.xnn SHORT_TFS empty / 300 / 0 / 4h,8h. load_dotenv(override=False) means any key dropped from the live EnvironmentFile silently activates the STALE .env value (e.g. re-enables 4h shorts).
structural_risks.money_impact: Silent knob flip on config edit/restart — e.g. shorts re-enabled or universe floor reinstated without any code change.

structural_risks.issue: open_positions() returns stale cache of UNBOUNDED age on every summary-fetch failure (exchange_nado.py:670-682). During a prolonged API outage the whole bot manages against a frozen positions view: phantom-guard blind (coins stay 'present' forever), trail SLs sized/placed for stale sizes.
structural_risks.money_impact: Deliberate anti-phantom-close bias; residual risk = management decisions on hours-old position data during outages.

structural_risks.issue: Universe loaded ONCE at startup (main.py:366, load_universe) and never refreshed — new listings untradeable and delisted coins scanned until the next restart; the coin→pid map for the WS feed is likewise startup-frozen (main.py:388).
structural_risks.money_impact: Missed edge on new listings; wasted scan budget on dead symbols.

structural_risks.issue: sqlite journal has no busy_timeout/WAL (journal.py:26-34, plain connect per op) while external scripts (backup_trades, post_trade_audit) touch the same DB. A 'database is locked' raise in insert_trade — which runs AFTER fill+SL (trader.py:617) — aborts attempt_entry: position live with SL resting but absent from memory AND DB → unmanaged (no trail) until manual fix; orphan-sweep exempts it (live position) so the SL at least survives.
structural_risks.money_impact: Untrailed but SL-protected position on a rare lock collision; no direct loss, edge decay only.

maps.bot: IB (ib_xnn_bot, US equities us29_trend + 38-contract futures tsmom38_m6ls; systemd ib-bot-v106.service → python -m bot.main, clientId=11, acct U16531055, TRADING_MODE=live DRY_RUN=0)
maps.entry_flow: File-driven, serial poll loop — NOT event-driven. bt-1 emits signal JSONs; systemd timer pulls them every 2min (systemd/ib-bot-signal-puller.timer OnUnitActiveSec=2min) into data/signals_inbox. Main loop: while-loop with MAIN_CYCLE_SEC=10 sleep (bot/main.py:363-376, .env MAIN_CYCLE_SEC=10). run_once (main.py:159): heartbeat→kill_switch.check→itertools.chain(pending_signals.scan, signal_reader.scan_inbox) (main.py:205) → trader.execute_signal serially per signal → trader.manage_open_positions (main.py:275). Throttles: B1 cap max 8 new ENTRIES/cycle (main.py:212, config.py:100); exits exempt but only for tsmom strategies (main.py:211 — us29 regime-flat NOT exempt, gets deferred). Capped/deferred us29+tsmom38 signals persist to data/signals_pending and re-scan every cycle rank-ordered (pending_signals.py:76-119). Session gates defer (not consume) via pending store (trader.py:862-864, 1112-1116). Blocking waits in entry path: A2 bracket confirm poll ≤3s/order (order_router.py:38-39,201-215), market-order fill wait ≤5s (order_router.py:937-940), A3 flat-confirm ≤3s (trader.py:1383-1393), per-signal live margin re-read ttl=0 (trader.py:248,894,1149). Worst-case cycle ≈8 entries×3s serial. No per-coin venue scan exists (signals precomputed on bt-1), so the Extended/HL scan-latency class does not apply; strategies are daily/monthly next-open so 10s cycle + 2min pull is adequate.
maps.order_path: Entry+SL is ATOMIC (A1): parent orderId pre-assigned via ib.client.getReqId(), every child gets parentId, transmit=True only on last child so SL activates with the entry — us29/tsmom38 use place_market_with_stop (order_router.py:1020-1044: MarketOrder parent transmit=False + StopOrder child transmit=True), legacy uk_v106 place_bracket STP-LMT parent + STP SL + LMT TP1 OCA (order_router.py:140-194). TP only on retired uk_v106 (50% LMT); active sleeves have no TP leg. Readback (A2): poll parent∈{PreSubmitted,Submitted,Filled} AND sl∈{PreSubmitted,Submitted} ≤3s (order_router.py:200-217, 1046-1058); on fail: cancel all legs, then status-triaged — parent Filled→emergency market close+global halt (order_router.py:240-247), Cancelled/Rejected→skip signal only (per-symbol, root-fix 2026-07-02 for Error-460 perm-rejects, order_router.py:231-251), ambiguous→global halt bias-to-safety (order_router.py:252-255). Post-fill A6 re-verify of SL deferred to next cycle via _pending_sl_verify flag (trader.py:1349, 1798-1806 → _verify_sl_after_fill trader.py:1513) with partial-fill qty reconcile (_reconcile_bracket_qty trader.py:1397, side-aware for tsmom38 shorts trader.py:1432-1456). Trail = A4 place-new-SL, confirm ≤2s, THEN cancel old (order_router.py:699-763) — zero naked window by design, but both stops live ≤2s and failed cancels are queued in state.stale_sl_oids for bounded retry (trader.py:1812-1836).
maps.state_model: Local SQLite data/trades.db is the bot's book: position_state keyed (coin,tf) JSON blob + processed_signals dedup + bot_state kv (bot/state_manager.py:58-77), trades journal (bot/journal.py:42-99). IB is trusted over DB for quantities: tsmom38 executor syncs held_qty from live position before computing deltas (trader.py:807-826), resize never writes held_qty until live convergence confirmed (trader.py:944-967). Reconciliation: (a) synchronous startup reconcile before main loop, critical→abort (main.py:341-351); (b) separate systemd timer every 5min running scripts/reconciler_once.py on clientId=12 (systemd/ib-bot-reconciler.timer + .service Environment=IB_CLIENT_ID=12). Heal matrix (bot/reconciler.py): short at IB→BUY force-flat (169-223); phantom at IB→halt only, no auto-flat (230-248); ghost in DB→record real exit from fills then delete state, gated by pending-entry grace (parent still resting, 260-281) and a time-based 2-strike all-empty-snapshot blip guard (99-125); qty mismatch→trust IB, patch state, side-aware stop resize (325-375); naked position→auto-place SL on held dated conId with IB-size clamp (525-646); post-roll-crash conId desync→adopt real SL (447-493); side-aware orphan SL/TP sweep with conId+mid-roll exemptions (648-748); cancelled journal rows resurrected on live match (289-302). Criticals trip RECONCILER_HALT sentinel (kill_switch.py:88-92) — blocks all new entries until operator clears.
maps.error_handling: Dominant pattern: broad `except Exception` + log + continue/fallback — 136 occurrences in bot/ (trader.py 37, order_router.py 29, exchange_ib.py 23, reconciler.py 16, main.py 12, others 19); ZERO bare `except:`. Money-touching gates are fail-closed: margin-gate exception→skip entry (trader.py:258-266, 901-904, 1156-1159); signal validation failure→CRITICAL log + quarantine, never trade (signal_reader.py:347-359). Failures that create naked risk fail-loud into sentinel halts (kill_switch.trip_reconciler at order_router.py:246,254,438,1092; trader.py:1607,1671). Weak spot: many heal/guard preambles degrade on exception with only a WARNING and 'proceeding' (e.g. anti-stack scan order_router.py:598-599, position pre-check 574-575), and cancel_order reports ok without confirming the cancel actually executed (exchange_ib.py:1067-1073).
maps.timeout_coverage: NO — IB API blocking calls are unbounded. ib_async IB() is created without ever setting RequestTimeout (exchange_ib.py:426; grep of bot/+scripts/ shows zero occurrences), so qualifyContracts (order_router.py:133, 331, 794; exchange_ib.py:583, 674, 957, 1009, 1039), reqHistoricalData (exchange_ib.py:681-686), accountSummary (exchange_ib.py:729), reqContractDetails (exchange_ib.py:589; trader.py:1910) all wait forever if the gateway accepts but never answers — and the bot is single-threaded, so one wedged call freezes trailing/exits/entries fleet-wide (exact sibling of the HL SDK hung-fetch root-fixed 2026-07-02). yf.download is called without an explicit timeout arg (data_source.py:250-253, 261-264, 369-373), relying on yfinance's internal default. Explicit timeouts that DO exist: ib.connect timeout=15 (exchange_ib.py:473); Telegram/Slack urlopen timeout=10 (notifier.py:53, 66); SMTP timeout=15 (notifier.py:86); watchdog systemctl subprocess timeout=10 (scripts/watchdog.py:40); all reqMktData snapshot waits are bounded poll loops (~2s, exchange_ib.py:884-890, 959-964).

structural_risks.issue: Kill/reconciler halt terminally CONSUMES entry signals: mid-cycle halt marks entries 'skipped_kill' via mark_signal_processed, and main.py then deletes the pending file (main.py:265-267) — tsmom38 is monthly, so a consumed rebalance entry is lost until month+1; us29 lost for a day. Known 2026-07-02 incident class, patch NOT present in this live snapshot (recovery lives with server-Claude, unlanded here).
structural_risks.money_impact: Missed monthly tsmom38 entries/resizes = up to a month of intended exposure lost per halted symbol; historic incident lost live trades (project_ib_skipped_kill_entry_loss_recovery)

structural_risks.issue: No ib_async RequestTimeout anywhere — single-threaded main loop hangs forever if gateway wedges inside qualifyContracts/reqHistoricalData/accountSummary/reqContractDetails; watchdog (scripts/watchdog.py) only ALERTS on stale last_ib_ok_ts, does not restart the unit.
structural_risks.money_impact: During a hang: no trailing, no max-hold/regime exits, no reconciler heals from the in-bot path; only resting GTC stops protect. Same class as HL hung-fetch (fixed on HL, latent here)

structural_risks.issue: A2 ambiguous-status after a failed bracket confirm trips the GLOBAL RECONCILER_HALT sentinel (order_router.py:252-255; also any reconciler critical, reconciler.py:750-753) — one symbol's transient glitch blocks ALL new entries until an operator manually clears the sentinel, and combined with the skipped_kill consumption above it converts a glitch into lost signals.
structural_risks.money_impact: Fleet-wide entry blackout of unbounded duration (historic ×124 perm-reject halt; perm-reject class fixed 07-02 but ambiguous-status and reconciler-critical paths still halt globally)

structural_risks.issue: cancel_order returns status=ok immediately after ib.cancelOrder() without confirming the cancel executed — modify_stop's 3-retry cancel-old loop (order_router.py:729-736) trusts this, so a gateway-rejected cancel is recorded as success: TWO live SELL-stops on one position with state believing one; §5 orphan sweep won't collect it (same conId as tracked position).
structural_risks.money_impact: Old stop fires first → new stop later market-sells a flat book → unintended short until the 5-min reconciler force-flats; round-trip slippage loss per occurrence

structural_risks.issue: Reconciler timer process runs on clientId=12 while orders belong to clientId=11 (systemd/ib-bot-reconciler.service ExecStart IB_CLIENT_ID=12); IB only allows canceling another client's orders from the master clientId — orphan-sweep and stale-SL cancels from the timer can be silently rejected (masked by the unverified cancel_order above) or persistently fail → rep.critical → global halt.
structural_risks.money_impact: Orphan protective orders rest live and fire into untracked positions; or repeated false criticals halt entries. Depends on gateway Master Client ID config (unverifiable from snapshot)

structural_risks.issue: open_positions() collapses multiple IB positions to ONE dict entry per canonical symbol (out[coin] overwrite) — two expiries on the same futures root (mid-roll crash window) make one leg invisible to ghost/naked/qty-sync checks.
structural_risks.money_impact: An invisible second-expiry leg can sit without SL and outside all heal paths until it moves against the account

structural_risks.issue: Phantom long at IB (position with no DB state) gets halt+alert only — the naked-SL heal (§4) iterates only db_states, so a genuinely acquired but untracked position runs with NO stop until an operator acts.
structural_risks.money_impact: Unbounded downside on the naked phantom during operator latency (mitigated by CRITICAL notify + entry halt)

structural_risks.issue: B1 entry-cap exit exemption covers only tsmom flat strategies (is_exit_sig excludes us29_trend) — a us29 regime-flat (SPY<SMA200, flatten whole book) arriving after 8 placements in a cycle is deferred to pending instead of executing immediately.
structural_risks.money_impact: Risk-off flatten delayed ≥1 cycle (10s+) in exactly the regime-break scenario where speed matters; bounded, small

structural_risks.issue: Legacy tsmom_12_1 chandelier trail computes highest_high over the FULL fetched 60-bar window including pre-entry bars (comment admits 'use all bars') — a pre-entry spike can place the stop at/above market and instantly exit a fresh position. Strategy retired (signal_reader whitelist) but code path is live for any surviving legacy state.
structural_risks.money_impact: Premature stop-out at a loss on legacy-held futures positions only; currently dormant
